title: A solid gold tile with a dark ruled void cut entirely through it, edge to edge. The gold field is the definition: complete, bounded, immovable. The ruled void is the precept itself — it cannot be filled because it runs the full depth of the material. You cannot undo a rule that is structural.
conceptualBridge: The ruled tile means Precept's law is carved into a fixed medium because the governing void cuts through the entire definition, making the rule impossible to close or override.
decision: Shortlisted as a radical departure that passes the rubric threshold; bold gold palette and carved-rule metaphor are completely new to the set.
